footer.tagline: Test your monitor for dead pixels, backlight bleed, and display problems. Free, no install.
footer.copyright: Free to use, no sign-up required.

hero.heading: Free Monitor & Screen Tests Online
hero.description: Test your display for dead pixels, backlight bleed, refresh rate, colour accuracy, and more. Works in any browser. No download, no sign-up.

index.title: Monitor Testing Blog
index.subtitle: Guides on display quality, calibration, pixel defects, and getting the most from your monitor tests.

fullscreenPanel.hint: Click or Space to advance · Arrow keys to navigate · Esc to exit

refreshRate.note: Result reflects the refresh rate your browser is currently using, not the hardware maximum.

resolution.note: Values auto-update on window resize and focus. Use Re-check after moving between monitors.

idle.heading: Click to Start
idle.description: The screen will turn green after a random delay. Click as fast as you can the moment it changes.

waiting.heading: Wait...
waiting.note: Do not click yet, wait for green

tooEarly.heading: Too Early
tooEarly.note: Wait for the screen to turn green, then click to try again

warning.title: Flashing imagery warning.
warning.body: This tool displays rapidly cycling colours at high frequency. Do not use it if you have photosensitive epilepsy, are sensitive to flashing or flickering light, or have been advised to avoid such content. If you experience any discomfort, stop immediately by pressing Esc or the Stop button.

stuckPixel.note: Run for 20 to 30 minutes for best results. Results are not guaranteed. This tool cannot fix dead pixels.

gamma.hint: Step back from your screen. The swatch that best matches the checkerboard indicates your display gamma.
gamma.fsHint: Step back and find which side matches · Click or Space to cycle reference · Esc to exit

warning.title: Flashing imagery warning.
warning.body: This tool displays rapidly cycling colours at high frequency. Do not use it if you have photosensitive epilepsy, are sensitive to flashing or flickering light, or have been advised to avoid such content. If you experience any discomfort, stop immediately by pressing Esc or the Stop button.

pwm.note: Flicker rate is relative to your monitor's frame rate. Real monitor PWM runs at 200-1000 Hz and is invisible to the eye - use the camera method to detect it.

hdr.note: Each step in the shadow and highlight ramps should be individually visible. Merged steps indicate crushed blacks or clipped highlights. True HDR luminance above SDR white cannot be rendered in a standard browser page.

caps.hdrSupported: Your browser and display report HDR capability.
caps.hdrUnsupported: Your browser or display does not report HDR support. An SDR display or a browser without HDR passthrough will show this.
caps.p3Supported: Your display can render colours beyond the sRGB gamut. P3 covers about 25% more colour than sRGB.
caps.p3Unsupported: Your display is reporting standard sRGB gamut. Most non-HDR monitors are sRGB only.
caps.rec2020Supported: Your display reports Rec. 2020 colour gamut, the broadcast HDR standard. This is rare on consumer monitors.
caps.rec2020Unsupported: Rec. 2020 not detected. This is expected on nearly all consumer monitors including most HDR displays.

affiliate.disclosure: As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases.
